name: Takoyaki Octopus Balls Japan
desc: Ball-shaped snacks made of a wheat flour-based batter and cooked in a special molded pan. A soul food staple of Osaka, Japan.

name: Oden Simmered Skewers Japan
desc: A one-pot winter dish consisting of several ingredients such as boiled eggs, daikon, and processed fishcakes stewed in a light, soy-flavored dashi broth in Japan.

name: Taiyaki Fish Cake Japan
desc: A classic street snack shaped like a sea bream. It is typically filled with sweet red bean paste, custard, or chocolate and served warm in Japan.

name: Ikanyaki Grilled Squid Japan
desc: Whole or sliced squid grilled over charcoal and brushed with a sweet and savory soy sauce glaze. A popular festival (matsuri) food in Japan.

name: Mitarashi Dango Glazed Dumplings Japan
desc: Skewered rice flour dumplings charred over a fire and dipped in a sweet soy sauce glaze. A traditional treat sold at shrines and temples in Japan.

name: Yakisoba Stir-Fried Noodles Japan
desc: Wheat noodles stir-fried with pork, vegetables, and a thick, sweet-savory sauce, topped with ginger and seaweed. A festival favorite in Japan.

name: Karaage Fried Chicken Japan
desc: Bite-sized chicken thigh marinated in ginger, soy sauce, and garlic, then deep-fried to a perfect crunch. A top-tier snack found at stalls across Japan.

name: Yaki-Tomokoroshi Grilled Corn Japan
desc: Fresh corn on the cob grilled and basted with soy sauce and butter. The aroma of this snack is iconic to summer festivals in Japan.

name: Choco Banana Dessert Japan
desc: A frozen banana dipped in chocolate and covered in colorful sprinkles. A visual and sweet delight for children at summer fairs in Japan.

name: Jaga-Butter Steamed Potato Japan
desc: A large, fluffy steamed potato served with a generous slab of melting butter. Simple, hot, and highly popular in the colder regions of Japan.

name: Okonomiyaki Savory Pancake Japan
desc: A 'Japanese pizza' or savory pancake containing a variety of ingredients, grilled on a teppan and topped with sauce and mayo in Japan.

name: Shaved Ice Kakigori Japan
desc: A summertime classic consisting of fluffy shaved ice flavored with colorful syrups. An essential way to cool down during the humid summer months in Japan.
